evidence: Contract action identity is split across packages: `goseg/protocol/contracts/protocol_contracts.go` binds contract actions from `actionids`, while `goseg/protocol/conformance/fixtures_test.go` validates fixtures against `protocol/actions` constants.

The conformance guard named `TestActionContractFixturesCoverCanonicalBindings` only hard-requires C2C presence and non-empty upload namespace checks, but does not assert one-to-one coverage for all action IDs defined in `goseg/protocol/actionids/actionids.go`.

`goseg/protocol/contracts/protocol_contracts.go` retains backward-compatible alias IDs (`UploadActionOpenEndpoint`, `UploadActionReset`) while specs and runtime IDs use newer names, and no governance/test rule in the assigned files enforces deprecation timelines or internal-only usage limits.

README quality-gate commands (`README.md`) require protocol contract/conformance tests but do not define an explicit architecture gate ensuring docs/runtime compatibility policy for alias retirement or cross-package action identity parity.

findings.summary: Protocol action identity is split across actionids, contracts, and action-facing tests, weakening boundary ownership and increasing cross-module drift risk.
evidence: `actionids.go` defines canonical raw values (`upload`, `c2c`, `open-endpoint`, `reset`, `connect`).
`protocol_contracts.go` wraps those same values into separate `ActionNamespace`/`ActionVerb` constants and then builds contract IDs from the wrapped layer.
`fixtures_test.go` validates fixture compatibility using `protocol/actions` symbols instead of the `actionids`/`contracts` vocabulary used by the contract catalog path.
`actions_test.go` asserts local upload action constants and contract map alignment, adding another package-specific coupling point for the same identifiers.
findings.suggestion: Establish one canonical protocol action authority package (typed namespace/action + ID derivation) and have contracts, conformance fixtures, and upload action tests consume that API directly; remove duplicated token wrappers and cross-package reinterpretation.

findings.summary: Backward-compatible alias IDs keep duplicate contract identities live in the same module, creating avoidable coupling and migration drag.
evidence: `protocol_contracts.go` exports both new IDs (`UploadOpenEndpointAction`, `UploadResetAction`) and old aliases (`UploadActionOpenEndpoint`, `UploadActionReset`) for identical contracts.
`family_specs_test.go` and related tests still reference alias-style IDs, which indicates architectural consumers are not converged on one naming boundary.
Maintaining parallel exported names for identical concepts prolongs module-level coupling because importers can bind to either path and impede cleanup sequencing.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a staged deprecation plan: designate one canonical exported ID family, migrate tests and consumers to it, and then remove alias exports in the next compatibility window to restore a single contract boundary.

findings.summary: Action identity authority is fragmented between `protocol/actions` and `protocol/actionids` without a direct parity contract.
evidence: `fixtures_test.go` validates fixture namespace/action against `groundseg/protocol/actions` symbols.
`protocol_contracts.go` defines contract namespace/action symbols from `groundseg/protocol/actionids` symbols.
No assigned-file test asserts that `actions` and `actionids` remain equivalent for the governed action set, so boundary drift can occur silently until a higher-level failure.
findings.suggestion: Establish one canonical action-ID authority (preferably a single package), then add a conformance test that verifies exact parity between runtime action constants and contract-bound action constants across all governed actions.

findings.summary: Current fixture conformance tests enforce partial presence checks instead of full canonical binding coverage.
evidence: `TestActionContractFixturesCoverCanonicalBindings` requires only one specific fixture (`c2c connect`) plus generic validation loop.
`TestValidateFixturesForNamespace` asserts upload fixture count is non-zero, not complete (both `open-endpoint` and `reset`).

Assigned canonical action set includes three actions in `actionids.go` and contract specs in `protocol_contracts.go`, but tests do not assert exhaustive equality between expected and fixture bindings.
findings.suggestion: Replace presence/count checks with an exact set-equality assertion between expected canonical bindings (derived from contract specs or action IDs) and fixture bindings, failing on both missing and extra entries.

findings.summary: Backward-compatible contract ID aliases are kept without an enforced retirement or containment policy, increasing long-term boundary ambiguity.
evidence: `protocol_contracts.go` exposes alias IDs (`UploadActionOpenEndpoint`, `UploadActionReset`) mapped to newer IDs.
`family_specs_test.go` still uses legacy alias naming in test setup (`UploadActionOpenEndpoint`), reinforcing dual-path internal usage.
README quality-gate and policy references do not define an explicit check that compatibility aliases are deprecated, constrained, or scheduled for removal.
findings.suggestion: Add a governance rule and test gate that marks alias contracts as compatibility-only (with deprecation metadata and sunset version), then prohibit new internal references to alias names outside compatibility adapters.
